LAW AND MOTION 302,DEFENDANTS JAY JAMBECK'S MOTION TO COMPEL THE DEPOSITIONS OF BOTH PLAINTIFFS WITH REQUEST FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS IS GRANTED IN PART. PLAINTIFFS' OWN OBJECTION AND OPPOSITION SHOW THAT THEY RECEIVED THE DEPOSITION NOTICE. PLAINTIFFS' OBJECTION TO THE DEPOSITION WAS UNTIMELY UNDER CCP 2025.410(B). ERRORS OR DEFECTS IN A DEPOSITION NOTICE ARE WAIVED UNLESS PROMPTLY CHALLENGED. (CCP 2025.410(A).) PLAINTIFFS SHALL APPEAR FOR DEPOSITION WITHIN 30 DAYS OF SERVICE OF THIS ORDER. THE PARTIES ARE TO MEET AND CONFER AS TO DATE AND PLACE OF DEPOSITIONS AND AGREE ON SAME WITHIN SEVEN DAYS OF SERVICE OF THIS ORDER. PLAINTIFFS MUST KEEP ENOUGH OPEN SPACE ON THEIR TELEPHONE-ANSWERING DEVICE TO RECEIVE MESSAGES. PLAINTIFFS MUST RETURN PHONE CALLS REGARDING DEPOSITION SCHEDULING WITHIN 24 HOURS. IF PLAINTIFFS FAIL TO COMPLY, DEFENSE COUNSEL MAY UNILATERALLY SET DEPOSITION DATES AND PLACES. IF MARIANNE CUNNINGHAM CLAIMS DISABILITIES THAT PREVENT HER FROM TRAVELING TO DEFENSE COUNSEL'S OFFICE, OR LIMIT HER TIME IN DEPOSITION, SHE MUST PROVIDE A WRITTEN DOCUMENT FROM A PHYSICIAN SPECIFICALLY STATING WHAT HER LIMITATIONS ARE. NO SANCTIONS ARE AWARDED. WRITTEN ORDER IS SIGNED AND FILED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: RICHARD ULMER; CLERK: JOSE RIOS MERIDA; REPORTER: REYES HUNTER, CSR #6576, BUS. TEL: 530-339-5023. (D302)

The court hears argument by plaintiff. Defendant submits. The Court confirms the tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS JAY JAMBECK'S Motion To Compel The Depositions Of Both Plaintiffs With Request For Monetary Sanctions is GRANTED in part. Plaintiffs' own objection and opposition show that they received the deposition notice. Plaintiffs' objection to the deposition was untimely under CCP 2025.410(b). Errors or defects in a deposition notice are waived unless promptly challenged. (CCP 2025.410(a).) Plaintiffs shall appear for deposition within 30 days of service of this order. The parties are to meet and confer as to date and place of depositions and agree on same within seven days of service of this order. Plaintiffs must keep enough open space on their telephone-answering device to receive messages. Plaintiffs must return phone calls regarding deposition scheduling within 24 hours. If plaintiffs fail to comply, defense counsel may unilaterally set deposition dates and places. If Marianne Cunningham claims disabilities that prevent her from traveling to defense counsel's office, or limit her time in deposition, she must provide a written document from a physician specifically stating what her limitations are. No sanctions are awarded. Written order is signed and filed in open court.

MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jun-02-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Plaintiff Marianne Cunningham telephone number 650-759-9457 appeared by telephone in Propria Persona.
Pamela Shafer of Rankin, Sproat, Mires, Reynolds, Shuey & Mintz telephone number 510-465-3922 for Defendants Jay Jambeck and Leigh Law Group.

The matter was argued. Upon conclusion of argument, the Court confirmed its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS JAY JAMBECK and LEIGH LAW GROUP'S MOTION TO STRIKE portions of the first amended complaint is granted as to the fourth cause of action for conversion and the prayers for attorney fees and denied as to all other portions of the first amended complaint sought to be stricken. While technically not permissible, plaintiffs' alleging of causes of action other than legal malpractice is not prejudicial to defendants since plaintiffs can seek leave to allege their new claims and, given the great liberality in allowing amendments to pleadings, would almost certainly be allowed to do so. The new claims for fraud, misrepresentation and accounting and new prayers for accounting are adequately pled, while the new claim for conversion and new prayers for attorney's fees and a constructive trust are not because they do not apply to the facts of this case. Plaintiffs have adequately alleged fraud, which is a sufficient predicate for their prayer for punitive damages. Ms. Cunningham is to provide a form of order.
